channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Environmental groups sue government to stop a big change to the Endangered Species Act
item.description: By altering the definition of the word "harm" as used by the Endangered Species Act, the Trump administration may limit how wildlife is protected in the United States. Environmental groups are suing.
item.content:encoded:
By altering the definition of the word "harm" as used by the Endangered Species Act, the Trump administration may limit how wildlife is protected in the United States. Environmental groups are suing.

(Image credit: Jared Lloyd)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: Investigative journalist reports on the abuse inside ICE's largest detention facility
item.description: The New Yorker writer Jonathan Blitzer says thousands of people are being held in tents in the El Paso desert, where inhumane conditions have become a tool to pressure people to accept deportation.
item.content:encoded: The New Yorker writer Jonathan Blitzer says thousands of people are being held in tents in the El Paso desert, where inhumane conditions have become a tool to pressure people to accept deportation.
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: Why your favorite international artist might be reconsidering their next U.S. tour
item.description: The visa process for visiting artists has always been complicated and expensive. Under the current administration, it's gotten significantly worse.
item.content:encoded: The visa process for visiting artists has always been complicated and expensive. Under the current administration, it's gotten significantly worse.
item.dc:creator: Josie Fischels

item.title: Inflation slowed sharply -- but it may not last
item.description: Annual inflation hit 3.5% in June, down from May's more than three-year high — but the resumption of the conflict with Iran threaten to push up inflation as energy costs once again spike.
item.content:encoded:
Annual inflation hit 3.5% in June, down from May's more than three-year high — but the resumption of the conflict with Iran threaten to push up inflation as energy costs once again spike.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: U.S. to reinstate Hormuz blockade. And, states sue over Paramount-Warner deal
item.description: The United States plans to reinstate a blockade over the Strait of Hormuz today. And, several states are suing to stop the massive Paramount-Warner Bros. merger.
item.content:encoded:
The United States plans to reinstate a blockade over the Strait of Hormuz today. And, several states are suing to stop the massive Paramount-Warner Bros. merger.

(Image credit: -)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Family of a man shot by agents responding to a 911 call seeks answers
item.description: A troubling pattern of federal agents fatally shooting civilians is developing, with deaths in Maine, Texas and Tennessee. The family of a man shot by agents told NPR they want answers.
item.content:encoded: A troubling pattern of federal agents fatally shooting civilians is developing, with deaths in Maine, Texas and Tennessee. The family of a man shot by agents told NPR they want answers.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: 'The Trojan Teddy Bear': The promise and peril of childhood in the age of AI
item.description: AI is moving beyond chatbots and into toys, dolls, and robots built to befriend children. A leading child-development expert says the technology offers real promise — but also risks crowding out the human relationships children need most.
item.content:encoded: AI is moving beyond chatbots and into toys, dolls, and robots built to befriend children. A leading child-development expert says the technology offers real promise — but also risks crowding out the human relationships children need most.
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: Trump scraps his Hormuz shipping charge idea but presses ahead with an Iran blockade
item.description: The U.S. military announced it will begin its blockade of Iranian ships over the Strait of Hormuz on Tuesday, as Iran vowed to assert its own control over the critical international waterway.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. military announced it will begin its blockade of Iranian ships over the Strait of Hormuz on Tuesday, as Iran vowed to assert its own control over the critical international waterway.

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Trump says the U.S. will collect tolls and impose blockade in the Strait of Hormuz, man killed by federal agents in Maine, states sue to stop Paramount-Warner Bros merger.
item.content:encoded: Trump says the U.S. will collect tolls and impose blockade in the Strait of Hormuz, man killed by federal agents in Maine, states sue to stop Paramount-Warner Bros merger.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Some states will ask voters to make it harder to pass constitutional amendments
item.description: Several states have ballot measures this year that could raise the thresholds needed to pass state constitutional amendments. Many advocates are critical of such limits on direct democracy.
item.content:encoded:
Several states have ballot measures this year that could raise the thresholds needed to pass state constitutional amendments. Many advocates are critical of such limits on direct democracy.

(Image credit: Kyle Rivas)
item.dc:creator: Ashley Lopez
